title: hieracium caesium subsp. rupicoloides
intro: Hieracium caesium subsp. rupicoloides, also known as mountain hawk's-beard, is a rare subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects, making it useful in treating conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and urinary issues. The plant has been traditionally used to promote digestive health, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems due to its expectorant properties. Hieracium caesium subsp. rupicoloides can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for its medicinal benefits, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for therapeutic purposes.
